User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the UniEats application, including customers, cafeteria staff, and administrators. Authentication details (passwords) are handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The institutional email address of the user, used for identification.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system (e.g., 'customer', 'cashier', 'kitchen_staff', 'admin').

universityId.description: The unique identifier assigned by the University UNI to the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a category for organizing menu items (e.g., 'Main Dishes', 'Beverages', 'Desserts').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., 'Bebidas Calientes', 'Ensaladas').

description.description: A brief description of the category.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the category, if available.

isActive.description: Indicates if the category is currently active and visible in the menu.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Represents a single food or beverage item available on the cafeteria menu.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

name.description: The name of the menu item.

description.description: A detailed description of the menu item.

price.description: The current selling price of the menu item.

imageUrl.description: URL to the image of the menu item.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this menu item belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N MenuItem)

isActive.description: Indicates if the menu item is currently active and visible in the menu.

preparationTimeMinutes.description: The estimated time in minutes required to prepare the item.

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the item is currently available for order (e.g., in stock).

stockQuantity.description: The current quantity of the item in stock for inventory management. (Optional, if not all items are quantity tracked)

calories.description: Nutritional information: total calories per serving.

proteinGrams.description: Nutritional information: grams of protein per serving.

fatGrams.description: Nutritional information: grams of fat per serving.

carbohydrateGrams.description: Nutritional information: grams of carbohydrates per serving.

allergens.description: A list of common allergens present in the menu item (e.g., 'gluten', 'nuts', 'dairy').

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's order for menu items, including details like pickup time, status, and total amount.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

pickupTime.description: The requested or estimated time for order pickup.

actualPickupTime.description: The actual time the order was picked up by the customer.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'preparing', 'ready_for_pickup', 'completed', 'cancelled').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including all items and any applicable taxes/fees.

pickupNumber.description: A unique number assigned to the order for virtual queue management and pickup identification.

paymentStatus.description: The status of the payment for the order (e.g., 'paid', 'pending', 'refunded').

cashierUserId.description: Reference to the User (staff) who processed this order at the POS, if applicable. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

cashRegisterSessionId.description: Reference to the CashRegisterSession during which this order was processed, if applicable. (Relationship: CashRegisterSession 1:N Order)

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single menu item included within an order, detailing quantity, price at time of order, and any customizations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem ordered. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the menu item ordered.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the menu item at the time of the order.

subtotal.description: The total cost for this specific order item (quantity * unitPrice).

notes.description: Any specific notes or simple customizations for this item (e.g., 'no onions', 'extra cheese').

customizations.description: A structured list of complex customizations applied to the menu item, if applicable (e.g., array of {option: 'size', value: 'large'}).

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Captures customer feedback, including ratings and comments, for menu items or overall service.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who submitted the review. (Relationship: User 1:N Review)

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem being reviewed, if applicable. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N Review)

orderId.description: Reference to the specific Order associated with the review, if applicable. (Relationship: Order 1:N Review)

rating.description: The numerical rating given (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: The textual comment or feedback provided by the user.

reviewDate.description: Timestamp when the review was submitted.

sentiment.description: The analyzed sentiment of the comment (e.g., 'positive', 'neutral', 'negative'), if an NLP service is used.

CashRegisterSession.title: CashRegisterSession
CashRegisterSession.description: Records the financial activity for a specific cashier's shift at a Point of Sale (POS) terminal, including cash movements and sales totals.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CashRegisterSession entity.

cashierUserId.description: Reference to the User (staff) who operated the cash register during this session. (Relationship: User 1:N CashRegisterSession)

startTime.description: Timestamp when the cash register session was opened.

endTime.description: Timestamp when the cash register session was closed.

openingCashAmount.description: The amount of cash in the drawer at the beginning of the session.

closingCashAmount.description: The amount of cash in the drawer at the end of the session, after reconciliation.

totalSalesAmount.description: The total sales amount recorded during this session (calculated or reconciled).

totalRefundsAmount.description: The total amount of refunds processed during this session.

discrepancyAmount.description: The difference between expected and actual cash at the end of the session.

status.description: The current status of the cash register session (e.g., 'open', 'closed', 'reconciled').

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles (customers, staff, admins). Only the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) can read or update their own profile. Includes denormalized 'role' for application logic, but security rules use dedicated role collections for DBAC.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Grants administrative privileges. The mere existence of a document at this path signifies that `adminId` is an administrator. Used for efficient Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC) in security rules.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of a user with administrative privileges.

definition.description: Grants cashier privileges. The mere existence of a document at this path signifies that `cashierId` is a cashier. Used for efficient Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC) in security rules.

params.name: cashierId
params.description: The unique identifier of a user with cashier privileges.

definition.description: Grants kitchen staff privileges. The mere existence of a document at this path signifies that `kitchenStaffId` is kitchen staff. Used for efficient Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC) in security rules.

params.name: kitchenStaffId
params.description: The unique identifier of a user with kitchen staff privileges.

definition.description: Defines categories for organizing menu items. Publicly readable for active categories. Only users with 'admin' or 'kitchen_staff' roles can create, update, or delete categories.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu item category.

definition.description: Stores individual food and beverage items, including nutritional information, pricing, and availability. Publicly readable for active items. Only users with 'admin' or 'kitchen_staff' roles can manage (create, update, delete) menu items.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the menu item.

definition.description: Stores customer orders. Includes denormalized `userId` (customer ID) for authorization independence. Customers can only read/update their own orders. Staff roles (cashier, kitchen_staff, admin) have broader access to read and update status based on their role permissions. Supports QAPs via `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order.

definition.description: Stores individual items within an order. Each document *must* include the denormalized `userId` (copied from the parent Order) to ensure Authorization Independence and avoid `get()` calls to the parent order in security rules. Access is tied to the parent order's permissions.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific item within the order.

definition.description: Captures customer feedback and ratings. Includes `userId` (reviewer ID) for authorization independence. Users can only create, update, or delete their own reviews. All users can read submitted reviews.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier of the review.

definition.description: Records the financial activity for cashier shifts. Includes denormalized `cashierUserId` for authorization independence. Only the owning cashier or an 'admin' can manage a session.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the cash register session.

definition.description: Stores personal nutritional consumption records for each user. This is private data, accessible only by the owning `userId`. Each log entry denormalizes the nutritional data from the corresponding MenuItem for fast lookups. The `NutritionLogEntry` entity schema is assumed to be defined as part of the application backend entities for this path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this nutrition log.

params.name: logEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific entry in the user's nutrition log.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for UniEats prioritizes Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control) to ensure a secure, scalable, and debuggable system. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
1.  **Denormalization of `userId` in `OrderItem`:** To avoid expensive and complex `get()` calls to the parent `Order` document within security rules for `OrderItem`s, each `OrderItem` document will explicitly include the `userId` of the customer who placed the order. This allows rules for `/orders/{orderId}/items/{orderItemId}` to directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without relying on `get(/databases/$(database)/documents/orders/$(orderId))`. This ensures atomic operations and simplifies rule logic considerably.
2.  **Role-based Access Control (DBAC):** Instead of checking a `role` field on the `User` document, which would require a `get()` call, user roles (`admin`, `cashier`, `kitchen_staff`) are implemented using dedicated collections (e.g., `/roles_admin/{uid}`). The *existence* of a document at these paths signifies a user's role, enabling efficient and atomic rule checks using `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`.

**Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security Posture:**
1.  **Publicly Accessible Data:** Collections like `/categories` and `/menuItems` are designed to be largely public (read access for `isActive: true` items), simplifying their security rules. Administrative access for these is granted via role-based checks.
2.  **User-Owned Private Data:** The `/users/{userId}` path ensures that user profiles are accessible only by their respective owners (`request.auth.uid == userId`). Similarly, `/users/{userId}/nutritionLog/{logEntryId}` segregates highly private consumption data, making it solely accessible to the authenticated user.
3.  **Collaborative/Shared Data (`orders`, `cashRegisterSessions`):** These collections contain data that needs to be accessed by different roles (customer, cashier, kitchen staff). Authorization logic within the rules will use the denormalized `userId` (for customer access) or the existence of role documents (for staff access) to grant permissions.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
This design naturally supports QAPs by ensuring that any `list` operation allowed by the rules can be efficiently fulfilled by the database engine without further server-side filtering. 
1.  **Customer Order History:** Customers query `/orders` using `firestore.collection('orders').where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. The security rules will explicitly allow `list` operations when the query includes this `userId` filter matching the authenticated user. This adheres to QAPs because the rule allows a query that *itself* enforces the necessary authorization predicate.
2.  **Staff Order Dashboard:** Staff with appropriate roles (e.g., `kitchen_staff`, `admin`) are granted `list` access to the entire `/orders` collection. They can then perform their own client-side or server-side filtering (e.g., by status, date) without violating QAPs, as the rule permits the full `list` operation for their role.
3.  **Public Menu & Categories:** `list` operations on `/categories` and `/menuItems` are generally allowed for all authenticated/unauthenticated users (filtered by `isActive: true`), meeting QAPs for public data.
4.  **Personal Nutrition Log:** `list` operations on `/users/{userId}/nutritionLog` are allowed only if `request.auth.uid == userId`, ensuring QAPs for private user-scoped subcollections.
